items.description: Linux package installation succeeds on WSL2 Ubuntu 24 with WSLg and required GTK/WebKitGTK development packages, without `EBADPLATFORM`, and produces an executable Linux helper binary.
steps: From WSL2 Ubuntu 24 with WSLg and required build packages installed, perform a clean install of the package from the repo or git source.
Verify install exits 0 and does not print `EBADPLATFORM`.
Verify the Linux helper binary exists at the documented package-local path and is executable.
items.regression_notes: 2026-03-13: Clean Linux npm install succeeded from a no-node_modules state without EBADPLATFORM. postinstall built .pi/extensions/generative-ui/native/linux/bin/pi-generative-ui-linux-helper and helper --version/self-test reported gtk3+webkit2gtk-4.1 on this WSL environment.

items.description: A headed interactive pi session can load the extension on Linux, open a native widget window, and receive a page message through `window.glimpse.send(...)`.
steps: Launch a headed interactive `pi` session with this package loaded from the local repo path.
Submit a prompt that triggers `visualize_read_me` and `show_widget`.
Verify a native Linux widget window opens and remains visible while the pi session stays active.
Interact with the widget so it sends a JSON payload through `window.glimpse.send(...)`.
Verify the tool result shown in pi includes the returned payload.

items.description: Streamed widget rendering updates appear incrementally in the headed pi session and finalize by executing deferred scripts once.
steps: In the headed interactive `pi` session, submit a visual prompt that causes a streamed `show_widget` call with visible partial updates.
Verify the widget window appears before the tool call is fully complete.
Verify at least one intermediate DOM update is visible before final completion.
Verify the finalized widget executes its deferred scripts after the final payload arrives.

items.description: Widget lifecycle resolution on Linux preserves manual close and abort behavior.
steps: Open a widget that does not immediately send a page message.
Manually close the widget window and verify pi reports the expected close result.
Open another widget, abort the active tool from pi, and verify the widget closes.
Verify no orphan helper process remains after each resolution path.

items.description: Linux diagnostics clearly distinguish missing display, missing helper binary, and missing runtime prerequisites.
steps: Exercise the extension with `DISPLAY` and `WAYLAND_DISPLAY` unavailable and verify the reported error names the missing GUI display.
Exercise the extension with the Linux helper binary missing or non-executable and verify the reported error names that specific condition.
Exercise the extension in a missing runtime/dependency scenario and verify the reported error includes actionable remediation guidance.

items.description: Package metadata and documentation describe Linux/WSLg support, installation requirements, and verification workflow without breaking existing pi package behavior.
steps: Review package metadata and README after the change.
Verify the documented install path matches how pi packages are loaded and installed.
Verify Linux prerequisites and the headed interactive pi verification workflow are documented for WSL2 + WSLg users.

items.description: The macOS Glimpse backend path remains intact and regression-verified in an appropriate macOS environment.
steps: In a macOS environment with the required toolchain, install the package and load it in pi.
Trigger a visual prompt that opens a widget through the Glimpse backend.
Verify the widget opens, can send a page message, and still follows the existing macOS lifecycle semantics.
